questions.question: When should you use Tableau/Power BI instead of Python?
options: When non-technical stakeholders need to explore data interactively, or for creating dashboards without coding
When the data has less than 1000 rows
questions.explanation: BI tools empower business users to self-serve. Python is better for analysis and custom charts; BI tools for shared live dashboards.

questions.question: A Tableau calculated field is:
options: A new column created by a formula applied to existing fields -- similar to a pandas computed column
questions.explanation: e.g. [Profit Ratio] = SUM([Profit]) / SUM([Sales]). Calculated fields create new measures or dimensions for your viz.

questions.question: In Tableau, the Marks card controls:
options: The visual encoding of data -- colour, size, shape, label, detail for the selected chart type
questions.explanation: Drag fields to Marks: Colour, Size, Label, Detail, Tooltip. This is the core of Tableau's visual encoding.

questions.question: Power BI's DAX (Data Analysis Expressions) is used for:
options: Creating calculated columns and measures -- similar to Excel formulas but for the data model
questions.explanation: DAX = formula language for Power BI measures. CALCULATE(), SUMX(), FILTER() are core DAX functions.

questions.question: Tableau's LOD (Level of Detail) expressions allow:
options: Computing aggregations at a different granularity than the current view -- e.g. customer-level avg inside a regional view
questions.explanation: FIXED [Customer]: SUM([Sales]) computes total sales per customer regardless of what level the viz shows.

questions.question: When choosing between Power BI and Tableau for enterprise:
options: Power BI: better if already using Microsoft/Azure ecosystem, cheaper. Tableau: stronger visualisation, steeper learning curve
questions.explanation: Power BI integrates natively with Azure, Teams, SharePoint. Tableau has richer chart types and more visual flexibility.

questions.question: A Tableau dashboard action (filter action) is used for:
options: Making one chart filter another when you click -- interactive cross-filtering between related charts
questions.explanation: Click a bar in chart A -> automatically filter chart B to show only that segment. Core interactive dashboard pattern.

questions.question: Extract vs Live connection in Tableau:
options: Extract: cached snapshot (fast, works offline). Live: real-time database query (always fresh, slower, requires connection)
questions.explanation: For dashboards with slow databases, extract weekly. For critical real-time KPIs, use live connection.

questions.question: The purpose of a KPI tile (card visual) in Power BI is:
options: Showing a single important metric prominently with target comparison -- e.g. Revenue: $1.2M (Target: $1.1M +9%)
questions.explanation: KPI tiles give immediate status at a glance. They anchor dashboards by showing the most important number first.

questions.question: Granularity in BI means:
options: The level of detail in your data -- daily vs monthly vs yearly aggregation -- affects all calculations
questions.explanation: Data at daily granularity lets you aggregate to monthly. Monthly data cannot be disaggregated to daily. Always keep the most granular data available.
